prompt: Using the provided reference photo as the source plant, transform it into a clean plant-identification infographic poster. Isolate the fuzzy green spherical Dianthus stems from the busy street/florist background, retouch them into a crisp studio botanical cutout, and place them on a soft pale green botanical layout with rounded information cards, leaf-line decorations, small green icons, and a trustworthy plant-app aesthetic. Use Simplified Chinese typography. Main headline: 绿毛球石竹; Latin name subtitle: Dianthus barbatus ‘Green Trick’; show a confidence badge at the upper right reading high-confidence identification. Add exactly 9 content panels: 1 top-left identification recommendation summary, 2 classification judgment, 3 key identification evidence, 4 similar plant distinction, 5 basic file, 6 growth habits and care, 7 risk warning, 8 how to confirm, and 9 bottom fun-fact strip. Keep the infographic text dense but neatly aligned, with numbered green circle markers for panels 2 through 8, icons matching each topic, and the plant image as the central visual focus. Plant identity to present: Dianthus barbatus ‘Green Trick’, also called Green Trick; family/genus line: Caryophyllaceae | Dianthus | sweet william / Dianthus barbatus horticultural cultivar.
